segments.text: Take a tile, and lay it before thee, and pourtray upon it the city, even Jerusalem
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Lay siege against it, and build a fort against it, and cast a mount against it; set the camp also against it, and set battering rams against it round about
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Take an iron pan, and set it for a wall of iron between thee and the city: and set thy face against it, and it shall be besieged, and thou shalt lay siege against it. This shall be a sign to the house of Israel
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: an iron pan: or, a flat plate, or, slice

segments.text: Lie upon thy left side, and lay the iniquity of the house of Israel upon it: according to the number of the days that thou shalt lie upon it thou shalt bear their iniquity
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I have laid upon thee the years of their iniquity, according to the number of the days, three hundred and ninety days: so shalt thou bear the iniquity of the house of Israel
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: When thou hast accomplished them, lie again on thy right side, and thou shalt bear the iniquity of the house of Judah forty days: I have appointed thee each day for a year
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: each day: a day for a year, a day for a year

segments.text: Set thy face toward the siege of Jerusalem, and thine arm shall be uncovered, and thou shalt prophesy against it
segments.speaker: Ezekiel

segments.text: I will lay bands upon thee, and thou shalt not turn thee from one side to another, till thou hast ended the days of thy siege
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: from one side: from thy side to thy side

segments.text: Take also wheat, and barley, and beans, and lentiles, and millet, and fitches, and put them in one vessel, and make thee bread thereof, according to the number of the days that thou shalt lie upon thy side, three hundred and ninety days shalt thou eat thereof
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Thy meat which thou shalt eat shall be by weight, twenty shekels a day: from time to time shalt thou eat it
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Thou shalt drink also water by measure, the sixth part of an hin: from time to time shalt thou drink
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Thou shalt eat it as barley cakes, and thou shalt bake it with dung that cometh out of man, in their sight
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Even thus shall the children of Israel eat their defiled bread among the Gentiles, whither I will drive them
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Ah Lord GOD! behold, my soul hath not been polluted: for from my youth up even till now have I not eaten of that which dieth of itself, or is torn in pieces; neither came there abominable flesh into my mouth
segments.speaker: Ezekiel

segments.text: Lo, I have given thee cow's dung for man's dung, and thou shalt prepare thy bread therewith
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will break the staff of bread in Jerusalem: and they shall eat bread by weight, and with care; and they shall drink water by measure, and with astonishment
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: That they may want bread and water, and be astonied one with another, and consume away for their iniquity
segments.speaker: God
